question: What is the origin of COVID-19 — zoonotic spillover or research-related lab leak?
shape: contested debate with multiple analysts reaching opposite conclusions from shared evidence

sources.title: Practically-A-Book Review: Rootclaim $100,000 Lab Leak Debate
sources.author: Scott Alexander
sources.role: core case material — the central synthesis of the debate

sources.title: Michael Weissman's Bayesian analysis of COVID origins
sources.author: Michael Weissman
sources.role: rigorous independent Bayesian analysis (example of a more formal method)

sources.title: COVID origins debate: Response to Scott Alexander
sources.author: Saar Wilf / Rootclaim
sources.role: the losing side's rebuttal — key for representing disagreement faithfully

known_crux_hint: Multiple sources indicate the zoonosis conclusion leans heavily on one claim: that the early case cluster at the Huanan Seafood Market is strong evidence for zoonosis. Removing/weakening it reportedly flips the overall conclusion. A successful crux-detection layer should surface this automatically from the graph — NOT be hand-coded to find it.
note: Debate video transcripts (Sessions 1-3) can be added as sources once transcribed; URLs in the competition brief.
